curriculum.title: Becoming an AI Operator
curriculum.subtitle: Mental model, Desktop Commander, first demos, Projects
curriculum.notes: Completed: Claude Max signup, Desktop Commander + Filesystem connectors installed, organized contracts/agreements folder, photo-to-structured-data (handwritten note digitized to DOCX), Google Calendar + Drive + Gmail connectors enabled, created AI Native Operator Training project in Cowork with CLAUDE.md, scheduled Session 2 via natural language calendar integration. Git for Windows install started but needs verification.
keyTakeaways: Claude Desktop with connectors = Claude has hands on your machine
Cowork mode handles desktop automation (file ops, browser, apps)
Projects + CLAUDE.md = persistent memory across conversations
Context window degrades past 75% — use session handoffs
Caps Lock = voice input for hands-free interaction

tasks.title: Install Git for Windows
tasks.description: Download from git-scm.com, install with defaults ('Add Git to PATH' stays checked), verify with 'git --version' in a fresh PowerShell window.

tasks.title: Verify Claude Code in terminal
tasks.description: Fresh PowerShell → 'claude --version'. If not recognized, run: irm https://claude.ai/install.ps1 | iex — then close and reopen terminal.

tasks.title: Build tip calculator (Code tab)
tasks.description: Claude Desktop → Code tab → 'Build a tip calculator app with bill splitting. Clean design, mobile-friendly. Save to Desktop and open in browser.'

tasks.title: Build tip calculator (Terminal)
tasks.description: PowerShell → mkdir $HOME\ai-demo → cd $HOME\ai-demo → claude → same prompt. Experience the terminal workflow difference.

tasks.title: Create 'HouseAccount Builder' project
tasks.description: Claude Desktop → Projects → New → 'HouseAccount Builder'. Add instructions describing HA, your role, team, the 4 agents, and that you're on Windows.

tasks.title: 3 real tasks per day in Claude
tasks.description: Provider emails, customer analysis, document review — actual HouseAccount work. Use Chat or Cowork, whichever feels natural.

tasks.title: Upload a dense document for analysis
tasks.description: Drop in a vendor contract, long email chain, or competitor's terms. Ask Claude to extract the 3 things that matter and flag anything unusual.

tasks.title: Try Caps Lock voice input
tasks.description: Hold Caps Lock → talk a real problem → let go. Use it for at least one actual task this week. Notice how different it feels.

resources.title: Windows PowerShell Quick Reference

mantras: The models are intelligence. When you're doing something that requires thinking, that's a signal to test a model with it.
Brain, Hands, Memory. Claude thinks. Claude Code acts. CLAUDE.md remembers.
If you're doing it more than twice, it should be a prompt. If it's a prompt you run more than twice, it should be automated.
Context is everything. A model with no context is smart but useless. A model with your business context is a teammate.
You're not learning a tool. You're building a new reflex.
The best AI operator isn't the best prompter. It's the person who knows what to hand off and what to keep.
